channel.title: The Zane Lowe Interview Series
channel.description: One thing that today's biggest artists have in common: They all speak with Apple Music’s Zane Lowe about their lives and the stories behind their songs. Hear why he is the interviewer the biggest stars open up to in these candid, in-depth conversations, now available in full on Apple Podcasts.
channel.itunes:author: Apple Music

item.title: Florence + the Machine
item.description: Florence Welch joins Zane discuss the new album ‘Dance Fever', breaking down tracks 'Morning Elvis', 'King', 'Free' and others. She talks about working with Jack Antonoff, her formative days in the South London indie scene and how her pandemic experiences changed the sound of the project.

item.title: Jack Harlow
item.description: Zane heads to Paramount Studios to catch up with Jack Harlow on his album 'Come Home The Kids Miss You'. They deep dive into the journey the artist has taken so far, his beginnings in Kentucky, the changing nature of his style and relationships with Drake, Lil Wayne and Pharrell. He also chats about his upcoming role in 'White men Can't Jump, his dynamic with fame and humilty and where he wants to go next.

item.description: Arcade Fire frontman, Win Butler, joins Zane in studio to talk about the bands latest album 'WE', channeling the ghost of David Bowie, his brother's departure from the band, and working with the legend Peter Gabriel.

item.title: Swedish House Mafia
item.description: Zane catches up with stadium behemoths and house music game-changers, Swedish House Mafia. Hot on the heels of their reunion, new album ‘Paradise Again’ and festival-headlining performances, DJ trio Steve Angello, Axwell and Sebastian Ingrosso speak on their time apart, influences at home, collaborations with A$AP Rocky and The Weeknd and reflect on their journey so far.

item.title: Bonnie Raitt
item.description: Zane travels to the San Fernando Valley and sits down with genre-melting icon, Bonnie Raitt, for an in-depth conversation into her expansive career and brand new album ‘Just Like That…’. From her days in Minneapolis with Prince to her memories of growing up around Los Angeles, Bonnie recounts the experiences that informed her latest body of work.

item.title: Jack White
item.description: Zane travels to Detroit to visit Jack White and his iconic Third Man Records headquarters. They sit down and talk about his upcoming album ‘Fear Of The Dawn’, explore the staggering vinyl pressing factory and stop by some iconic locations integral to White Stripes lore.

item.description: Charli XCX joins Zane in studio to talk about her album ‘CRASH’. They cover her growth throughout her career, how pop music has had to catch up to her, the impact SOPHIE had on her life, and much more.

item.description: Zane and ROSALÍA sit down over churros to discuss the journey to making her third album ‘MOTOMAMI’. She discusses her relationships with family, privacy and growth and collaborations with Pharrell and The Weeknd, alongside exploring the differences between LA and Barcelona.

item.title: Questlove
item.description: Fresh from his Oscar’s nomination, Zane chats to Questlove about his documentary ‘Summer of Soul (...Or, When the Revolution Could Not Be Televised)’, the significance of 1969, ‘code switching’ of black artists, how the festival was almost erased from history and teases his new Sly and the Family Stone project.

item.description: Roland Orzabal and Curt Smith, duo of archetypal band Tears For Fears, join Zane to talk about their latest album 'The Tipping Point’, reuniting with one another, being sampled by The Weeknd, Drake and Kanye and reflect on their iconic hits.

item.title: Marilyn Manson
item.description: “I bet people could probably say, ‘I’ve never seen Manson in a good mood once in my life.’ Except here right now.” In this in-depth interview, Marilyn Manson tells Zane Lowe all about the making and meaning of his 11th album, WE ARE CHAOS. The pair discuss his collaboration with Shooter Jennings, 2020 not quite going as expected, and the influence of grief and his relationship with mental health.
item.itunes:title: Marilyn Manson
item.itunes:summary: “I bet people could probably say, ‘I’ve never seen Manson in a good mood once in my life.’ Except here right now.” In this in-depth interview, Marilyn Manson tells Zane Lowe all about the making and meaning of his 11th album, WE ARE CHAOS. The pair discuss his collaboration with Shooter Jennings, 2020 not quite going as expected, and the influence of grief and his relationship with mental health.
item.content:encoded: “I bet people could probably say, ‘I’ve never seen Manson in a good mood once in my life.’ Except here right now.” In this in-depth interview, Marilyn Manson tells Zane Lowe all about the making and meaning of his 11th album, WE ARE CHAOS. The pair discuss his collaboration with Shooter Jennings, 2020 not quite going as expected, and the influence of grief and his relationship with mental health.

item.title: Alicia Keys
item.description: In a far-reaching interview with Zane, Alicia Keys discusses the ways in which her childhood and the things she had to overcome led to the clarity of her latest album ALICIA, her first in four years. She offers a deep dive into the mindset behind and the making of the music, which is some of her most expansive and collaborative to date. “There’s something so crazy about what I bring and then what you bring. It’s going to be completely different, and there’s something about the merging of energy that’s fascinating to me,” she says. “Now I feel like that’s another alchemy that’s created that I love the process of—that process of being open and receiving what’s meant to come from each person and creating something out of nothing is always incredible.” The conversation, much like the album, is as vulnerable as it is refreshing.

item.title: Katy Perry
item.description: Days before releasing her sixth album Smile—and before giving birth to her first child—the glowing global superstar opens up about the perseverance it took to get there. “The last two and a half years have been a psychological, spiritual, emotional journey,” she tells Zane Lowe. “I'm grateful for the pain because it pushed me towards the growth. Now I don’t feel like a thirsty, desperate pop star that's just trying to hit a number. I've got more dimension. I have a baby on the way. I have a fiancé. I love my nieces. I love my soon-to-be stepson. I love life.”

item.description: In his powerful interview with Zane Lowe, the theater performer, singer, and actor opens up about finding his voice at a young age, coping with and overcoming trauma, and how playing Belize in Tony Kushner’s off-Broadway revival of Angels in America changed his life.

item.title: Lady Gaga
item.description: Ahead of the release of Chromatica, Lady Gaga sat down for a socially distanced chat with Apple Music’s Zane Lowe. In a wide-ranging conversation, they discussed making music as a form of therapy (“my way of dealing with pain is that I write songs”), working with collaborators like Ariana Grande (“this friendship blossomed”), and what she plans to do after the quarantine is lifted (“I’m probably going to go to every gay club that I can find, and hug and kiss every human that I come in contact with”).

item.description: To mark the release of Changes, his first album since 2015’s Purpose, Justin Bieber opens up to Apple Music’s Zane Lowe about how his faith and his relationship with new wife Hailey Baldwin saved his life and inspired him to make music again. (2/15/2020)

item.title: Kanye West, Part 2
item.description: In part two of Zane Lowe’s wide-ranging interview with Kanye West at his ranch in Wyoming, the rapper gets candid about his struggles with mental health. He explains how the changes he made in his personal life are reflected in the making of JESUS IS KING, his first release since fully embracing Christianity. (10/24/2019)

item.title: Kanye West, Part 1
item.description: On October 22, 2019, Zane Lowe traveled to Kanye West’s ranch in Cody, Wyoming. On the eve of the release of his ninth solo project JESUS IS KING, West talked openly about his newfound faith, his hopes and fears for the future, and his entrepreneurship—including his efforts to build sustainable manufacturing in the US for his Yeezy brand. (10/24/2019)
